title: Accessible Puzzle Interface and Multichannel Feedback · Runtime Lens: Accessible Controls
summary: Walls, visited cells, current focus, route, and errors are communicated only by color and animation, while the Canvas has no usable alternative. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the accessible controls increment in keyboard-first visual and structured-text maze interface. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence de…
objectives: Provide keyboard navigation, remapping, visible focus, large targets, clear labels, no traps, and announced state changes.
Communicate wall, focus, visited, frontier, route, success, and error through redundant text, shape, pattern, position, and optional sound.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 3340 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile visual maze state with structured text, keyboard control, multichannel cues, and barrier closure; all graph claims need an independent witness; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Provide keyboard navigation, remapping, visible focus, large targets, clear labels, no traps, and announced state changes. A defensible solution applies Accessible Controls, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to keyboard-first visual and structured-text maze interface.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-accessible-interface-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-accessible-interface-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_accessible_interface_2(essential_states: int = 9, text_equivalents: int = 9, keyboard_actions: int = 9, color_only_states: int = 0, open_barriers: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if essential_states < 1 or text_equivalents < 0:
        return False, text_equivalents, "accessibility-count"
    if text_equivalents != essential_states or keyboard_actions < essential_states:
        return False, text_equivalents, "equivalent-experience"
    if color_only_states > 0:
        return False, color_only_states, "color-only-cue"
    if open_barriers > 0:
        return False, open_barriers, "unclosed-barrier"
    assert text_equivalents == essential_states
    return True, text_equivalents, "multichannel-maze-interface"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Build Accessible Controls evidence · Theory Accessible Controls

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-accessible-controls-1mqvx6
# Competency: maze-accessible-controls.
# Case 2642: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: event-loop work must remain cancellable.
# Changed case: inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it.
def evidence_maze_accessible_controls_1mqvx6(essential_states: int = 9, text_equivalents: int = 9, keyboard_actions: int = 9, color_only_states: int = 0, open_barriers: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1mqvx6 = "1mqvx6-6xvqm1-2642"
    assert evidence_variant_1mqvx6
    evidence_axes_1mqvx6 = (
        "grid1mqvx6",
        "topology2642",
        "search6xvqm1",
        "event1mqvx62642",
        "access26421mqvx6",
        "recovery6xvqm12642",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1mqvx6)) == 6
    if essential_states < 1 or text_equivalents < 0:
        return False, text_equivalents, "accessibility-count"
    if text_equivalents != essential_states or keyboard_actions < essential_states:
        return False, text_equivalents, "equivalent-experience"
    if color_only_states > 0:
        return False, color_only_states, "color-only-cue"
    if open_barriers > 0:
        return False, open_barriers, "unclosed-barrier"
    assert text_equivalents == essential_states
    return True, text_equivalents, "multichannel-maze-interface"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Accessible Controls: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory puzzle team handles case 1530 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile visual maze state with structured text, keyboard control, multichannel cues, and barrier closure; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Provide keyboard navigation, remapping, visible focus, large targets, clear labels, no traps, and announced state changes.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Accessible Controls model · Theory Accessible Controls.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Accessible Controls model · Theory Accessible Controls, keep Accessible Controls connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Provide keyboard navigation, remapping, visible focus, large targets, clear labels, no traps, and announced state changes.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Accessible Controls model · Theory Accessible Controls, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Accessible Controls precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Accessible Controls model · Theory Accessible Controls, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 1530 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile visual maze state with structured text, keyboard control, multichannel cues, and barrier closure; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Provide keyboard navigation, remapping, visible focus, large targets, clear labels, no traps, and announced state changes.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Accessible Controls model · Theory Accessible Controls, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Native Tk widgets are automatically accessible in every composition.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-accessible-controls-1mqvx6.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-accessible-controls-1mqvx6(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_accessible_controls_1mqvx6\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?essential_states < 1 or text_equivalents < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?text_equivalents != essential_states or keyboard_actions < essential_states)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?color_only_states > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?open_barriers > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
